Politics.headline: PM meeting with President of France, Emmanuel Macron and NATO Secretary General, Mark Rutte: 4 May 2026
Politics.detail: The Prime Minister of the UK met with the President of France and the NATO Secretary General at the European Political Community Summit to discuss the situation in Ukraine and the Middle East, particularly the need to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and restore freedom of navigation. The leaders agreed on the need to reopen of the Strait of Hormuz to restore freedom of navigation and the free flow of global trade.

Politics.headline: NATO chief says Europeans have 'gotten the message' from Trump on defence
Politics.detail: The NATO chief stated that Europeans have understood Trump's message regarding defense. This likely refers to increased defense spending or burden-sharing within the alliance, a recurring theme in international political discussions.

Politics.headline: Republicans split with Trump and back Haitians – to save their seats
Politics.detail: Republicans are diverging from Trump's policies and supporting Haitians, potentially as a strategy to protect their seats. This suggests a shift in political alignment or a response to changing demographics and voter sentiment.

Politics.headline: PM meeting with Prime Minister Pashinyan of Armenia: 4 May 2026
Politics.detail: The Prime Minister of the UK met with the Prime Minister of Armenia to commend Armenia's steps to secure regional peace and to agree to a new UK-Armenia Strategic Partnership. This partnership aims to strengthen cooperation on defence and security, economic growth, and democratic resilience.

Politics.headline: Official Visit of Prime Minister of New Zealand Christopher Luxon, 4 May 2026
Politics.detail: The Prime Minister of Singapore and the Prime Minister of New Zealand held the inaugural Singapore-New Zealand Annual Leaders' Meeting, reaffirming their longstanding relations. They also signed an Agreement on Trade in Essential Supplies (AOTES), the world's first legally-binding bilateral supply chain resilience agreement.

Finance & Economy.headline: GameStop makes $55.5bn takeover offer for eBay
Finance & Economy.detail: GameStop has made a $55.5 billion takeover offer for eBay, signaling a major shift in the retail landscape. The move could create a dominant player in both online and physical retail spaces.

Finance & Economy.headline: UK food prices on track to rise by 50% since start of cost of living crisis
Finance & Economy.detail: A new report indicates that food prices in the UK are expected to increase by 50% since the beginning of the cost of living crisis, exacerbating financial pressures on households. This surge in prices is driven by various factors, including supply chain disruptions and inflation.

Finance & Economy.headline: Spirit Airlines shutdown: How to get home and get refunds
Finance & Economy.detail: Spirit Airlines has ceased operations, leaving many passengers stranded. Affected travelers are seeking information on how to secure alternative transportation and obtain refunds for their cancelled flights.

Finance & Economy.headline: US national debt surpasses size of the economy for first time since World War II
Finance & Economy.detail: The US national debt has exceeded the size of the country's economy for the first time since World War II. This development raises concerns about long-term fiscal sustainability and potential economic consequences.

Finance & Economy.headline: Oil Surges Over 3%
Finance & Economy.detail: Oil prices jumped more than 3% on Monday, following reports that two missiles struck a US warship after it ignored Iranian warnings, though a senior US official denied the strike. The IRGC Navy also released a new map, designating all areas of the Strait of Hormuz under Iranian military control.

Technology.headline: Kyndryl drives AI-led banking modernization in Luxembourg
Technology.detail: Kyndryl is extending its partnership with Banque Internationale à Luxembourg (BIL) to modernize the bank's IT infrastructure, accelerate the adoption of AI and cloud technologies, and strengthen operational and cyber resilience. This collaboration aims to enhance customer experience and create a more agile technology framework for the bank.

Technology.headline: Autonomous aircraft boost logistics reach
Technology.detail: The Air Force's Jericho Disruptive Innovation (JDI) team is advancing autonomous aerial logistics systems to support agile operations. Project Camel Train is prototyping JabX, an uncrewed aerial system, to link remote airfields and coastal bases, while Project Arena focuses on enabling these aircraft to navigate crowded airspace safely.

Technology.headline: Georgia Tech students build future of driving through national competition
Technology.detail: Georgia Tech students are participating in the EcoCAR EV Challenge, a national competition where universities reimagine electric vehicles. This year, they are modifying a 2023 Cadillac Lyriq to improve its performance and prepare it for the final round of the competition.

Technology.headline: Tower Semiconductor to Participate in May and June Investor Conferences
Technology.detail: Tower Semiconductor, a leading foundry of high-value analog semiconductor solutions, announced its participation in several investor conferences in May and June.  The company will be present at conferences in Tel Aviv, New York, and Minneapolis to meet with investors.

Technology.headline: BN Daybreak - Mon 4 May 2026: Deepfake scams, Big Tech's AI boom, and US intervention in the Pers...
Technology.detail: BizNews Daybreak covers a deepfake investment scam, the AI boom driving revenue growth for Google, Meta, and Amazon, and Tinder's introduction of mandatory facial recognition for new users. The report highlights AI's impact on cloud revenues and infrastructure spending, as well as efforts to combat misuse of technology in scams and dating apps.

Science.headline: Creatine powers cells and regenerates ATP
Science.detail: Creatine, naturally produced in the body, helps power cells by rapidly regenerating ATP, the fuel that keeps muscles, the brain, and even the heart running during intense activity.

Science.headline: Blood test may soon detect depression
Science.detail: A new study suggests depression may soon be detectable through a simple blood test by tracking how certain immune cells age, and researchers found that accelerated aging in monocytes, a type of white blood cell, is closely tied to emotional and cognitive health.

Science.headline: Cranberry juice may boost UTI antibiotics
Science.detail: Cranberry juice may enhance the effectiveness of antibiotics used to treat urinary tract infections.

Science.headline: Ancient Egyptian Mummy Found Wrapped In Something Never Seen Before
Science.detail: An ancient Egyptian mummy was discovered wrapped in a material that has not been seen before.

Science.headline: Newly mapped brain networks link far-flung regions
Science.detail: In mouse brains, star-shaped astrocytes form flexible networks that may offer another way for brain regions to communicate.

Health.headline: Kirsty, 11, seeks more Kirstys to help raise money for brain tumour research
Health.detail: An 11-year-old girl named Kirsty is looking for other people named Kirsty to help her raise money for brain tumor research.

Health.headline: Who is Nicole Saphier, Trump's new nominee for US surgeon general?
Health.detail: An article discusses Nicole Saphier, who is nominated by Donald Trump for US surgeon general.

Health.headline: London schools trialling VR to relieve pupils' stress
Health.detail: London schools are testing virtual reality to help students relieve stress.

Health.headline: GLP-1 Drugs May Modestly Raise Optic Neuropathy Risk in T2D
Health.detail: GLP-1 drugs, commonly used for type 2 diabetes, may slightly increase the risk of optic neuropathy.

Health.headline: Amoxicillin Commonly Used for Ear Infections in Finnish Kids
Health.detail: A recent report indicates that amoxicillin is frequently prescribed for ear infections in children in Finland.

Sports.headline: Golden Tempo takes the Kentucky Derby as Cherie DeVaux becomes the first woman to train its winner.
Sports.detail: Golden Tempo charged past the field down the stretch to win the Kentucky Derby, making Cherie DeVaux the first woman to train the winner of the race. Attention now turns to the Preakness Stakes on May 16, with DeVaux saying a decision on Golden Tempo's participation will be made in the coming days.

Sports.headline: Genius Sports and Liga MX strike landmark technology and AI partnership to drive future of Mexican soccer
Sports.detail: Liga MX teams will be equipped with Genius Sports' Performance Studio, providing coaches and analysts access to performance and analytics tools to enhance training, tactics, and player development. The partnership aims to create real-time sponsor inventory and unique broadcast experiences alongside world-leading semi-automated offside technology.

Sports.headline: Clinical Inter Milan secures Italian Serie A title as rivals stumble in one-sided race
Sports.detail: Inter Milan has clinched the Italian Serie A title, marking their dominance in a season where their rivals faltered. This victory underscores Inter Milan's strong performance throughout the season.

Sports.headline: Jury selection to begin in former Patriots receiver Stefon Diggs' assault trial.
Sports.detail: The trial for former New England Patriots wide receiver Stefon Diggs is set to begin with jury selection. Diggs is facing charges related to an in-game altercation with a Detroit Lions fan.

Sports.headline: Cale Makar returns from injury, scores twice as Avalanche outlast Wild 9-6 in wacky Game 1.
Sports.detail: Cale Makar made a comeback from injury and scored two goals as the Avalanche defeated the Wild 9-6 in Game 1. Makar's performance highlighted his impact on the team's success.

Entertainment.headline: Cameron Diaz & Benji Madden Welcome Baby #3 — Find Out His Unique Name!
Entertainment.detail: Cameron Diaz and Benji Madden announced the birth of their third child, a boy named Nautas, with Benji sharing the news and the meaning of the name on Instagram. The couple also has a daughter, Raddix, 6, and a son, Cardinal, 2.

Entertainment.headline: Princess Eugenie and her husband announce they are expecting their 3rd child
Entertainment.detail: Princess Eugenie and her husband, Jack Brooksbank, are expecting their third child this summer, with their sons August, 5, and Ernest, 2, excited about the new sibling. King Charles III is reportedly delighted by the news.

Entertainment.headline: 'The Devil Wears Prada' struts to first place with US$77 million debut
Entertainment.detail: Twenty years after the original, the sequel to “The Devil Wears Prada” made a splash in its first weekend in theaters, earning US$77 million. The film also includes cameos from Anna Wintour, Naomi Campbell and Lady Gaga.

Entertainment.headline: Shakira to follow Madonna and Lady Gaga in giving a huge free concert on Copacabana Beach
Entertainment.detail: Colombian superstar Shakira will perform a free concert on Copacabana Beach in Rio de Janeiro on Saturday night, with Rio officials expecting approximately 2 million attendees. This follows similar performances by Madonna and Lady Gaga.

Entertainment.headline: What celebrity worship says about self-worth
Entertainment.detail: A new study from Flinders University suggests that people with a less defined sense of self may look to celebrities to help find their identity. The research indicates that celebrity admiration is linked to personal direction, autonomy, and self-understanding rather than fame itself.

Crime & Justice.headline: Shooting at lake near Oklahoma City injures at least 10
Crime & Justice.detail: A shooting at a party at a lake near Oklahoma City sent more than a dozen people to hospitals, according to police and hospital officials.

Crime & Justice.headline: Australian Jews tell antisemitism inquiry of surge in hate before Bondi Hanukkah massacre
Crime & Justice.detail: An Australian inquiry into antisemitism has heard from Jewish Australians who feel increasingly fearful after a massacre at a Hanukkah celebration. All witnesses called to give evidence on Monday were Jewish Australians who recounted their experiences of hatred, some speaking under pseudonyms out of fear for their safety.

Crime & Justice.headline: Matt Hancock 'assaulted on London Underground' as man arrested
Crime & Justice.detail: UK politician Matt Hancock was reportedly assaulted on the London Underground, and a man has been arrested in connection with the incident.

Crime & Justice.headline: Britney Spears is set to be arraigned on a DUI charge. But she doesn't have to appear in court.
Crime & Justice.detail: Britney Spears is scheduled to be arraigned on a DUI charge but is not required to appear in court.

Crime & Justice.headline: Suspect in Long Island stabbings killed 1 coworker, 1 roommate, police say
Crime & Justice.detail: A suspect in custody after a stabbing incident on Long Island is said to have killed one coworker and one roommate, according to police.

Education.headline: Austin ISD student dyslexia rate jumps to nearly 23%
Education.detail: More than 1 in 5 Austin ISD students are now identified as dyslexic, which is higher than any other major urban Texas school district, and district leaders attribute the surge to state policy changes, improved identification, and stronger educator training.

Education.headline: What else does Florida's new anti-teacher union law include?
Education.detail: Florida’s Governor Ron DeSantis signed a bill that further regulates teacher unions, allowing districts to give teacher incentives at low-performing schools without collective bargaining, clarifies rules for student use of epinephrine devices, and permits middle schoolers to earn CAPE Digital Tools certificates.

Education.headline: JEE Main 2026 Paper 2 Final Answer Key Out, Check B.Arch And B.Planning Responses Now
Education.detail: The final answer key for JEE Main 2026 Paper 2, which covers B.Arch and B.Planning, has been released.

Education.headline: Our Stories in Action: Peel District School Board celebrates Education Week
Education.detail: Peel District School Board (PDSB) is celebrating Education Week from May 4 to 8, 2026, with the theme “Our Stories in Action,” recognizing contributions to public education and highlighting student achievements.

Education.headline: Sudanese schoolchildren race to make up for years lost to war
Education.detail: Afrah, a 13-year-old Sudanese girl displaced by war, is determined to become a surgeon and continues her education at a displacement camp in Port Sudan with the help of UNICEF and SCEFA.

Lifestyle.headline: Met Gala 2026 LIVE Updates: From Beyoncé's return after a decade to Karan Johar's debut, all eyes are on the iconic Met steps
Lifestyle.detail: The Met Gala 2026 is happening, with updates covering Beyoncé's anticipated return after ten years and Karan Johar's first appearance.

Lifestyle.headline: 49 ways to have fun right now! Skydive in a wind tunnel, count dogs and run like a three-year-old
Lifestyle.detail: This article lists various fun activities to engage in, including skydiving in a wind tunnel, counting dogs, and running like a child.

Lifestyle.headline: Georgina Hayden's quick and easy recipe for spanakopita orzo
Lifestyle.detail: Georgina Hayden shares a simple recipe for spanakopita orzo, providing a quick and easy meal idea.

Lifestyle.headline: Priyanka Chopra To Skip Met Gala In New York, To Be Honoured In Los Angeles
Lifestyle.detail: Priyanka Chopra will not attend the Met Gala in New York but will instead be honored in Los Angeles.

Lifestyle.headline: 'He should not look like a Greek god': The 'shocking' 16-month transformation that turned Ram Charan into a 'Desi Pehalwan'
Lifestyle.detail: This article details Ram Charan's 16-month transformation into a 'Desi Pehalwan,' emphasizing a specific approach to his physical appearance.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial cartoons for the week of May 4
Opinion/Editorial.detail: A collection of editorial cartoons providing visual commentary on the week's news.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Smiles and smirks: Editorial cartoons from the past week (May 4, 2026)
Opinion/Editorial.detail: A compilation of editorial cartoons offering satirical takes on recent events.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Wilson: A heavy heart for the Sayers saga after the conviction of an anti-violence activist
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The author reflects on the conviction of Lumumba Sayers and the tragic consequences for both the Watson and Sayers families, highlighting the complexities and sorrows surrounding the case.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial cartoons for the week of May 4
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This article presents editorial cartoons offering visual commentary on current events.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: 'Corporate Meddling and Editorial Fear'
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This article discusses the concerns of CNN journalists regarding potential mass layoffs and the loss of editorial integrity due to a possible acquisition of WBD by Paramount.

World.headline: US denies Iran claim that warship struck by missiles as tensions rise in Strait of Hormuz
World.detail: The U.S. has denied claims from Iran that a U.S. warship was struck by missiles in the Strait of Hormuz, amidst rising tensions in the region. The U.S.-led Joint Maritime Information Center has advised ships to cross the strait in Oman's waters, establishing an enhanced security area.

World.headline: Three passengers dead after suspected hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship
World.detail: Three passengers have died, and several others sickened, following a suspected hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship in the Atlantic. The cruise ship was initially blocked from docking in Cape Verde due to the outbreak.

World.headline: US military says two service members taking part in Morocco drills missing
World.detail: Two U.S. service members are missing in southwestern Morocco after participating in annual multinational military exercises. The missing service members are US Army soldiers who went missing while on a hike.

World.headline: Iran executes three men in relation to January anti-regime protests
World.detail: Iran has executed three men for their involvement in anti-regime protests that took place in January. This comes amid heightened tensions in the region and international scrutiny of Iran's human rights record.

World.headline: PM meeting with President of France, Emmanuel Macron and NATO Secretary General, Mark Rutte: 4 May 2026
World.detail: The Prime Minister of the UK met with the President of France and the NATO Secretary General at the European Political Community Summit to discuss the situation in Ukraine and the Middle East, agreeing on the need to reopen the Strait of Hormuz to restore freedom of navigation and the free flow of global trade. They also discussed European support in the region to reinforce security.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe eyes new wheat record
Zimbabwe.detail: In the 2025 season, Zimbabwe produced more than 639 000 tonnes of wheat, surpassing the country's annual consumption demand of 615 000 tonnes, and is looking to break that record this year.

Zimbabwe.headline: US$1m youth fund rollout begins
Zimbabwe.detail: The rollout of the US$1 million youth fund has begun.

Zimbabwe.headline: World Bank appoints Firas Raad as Zim division head
Zimbabwe.detail: The World Bank Group has appointed Firas Raad as Division Director for Tanzania, Malawi, Zambia and Zimbabwe, effective May 1.

Zimbabwe.headline: US$12m investment to create jobs in Bulawayo
Zimbabwe.detail: An investment of US$12 million is expected to create jobs in Bulawayo.

Zimbabwe.headline: Govt begins process of blending diesel amid fuel cost pressures
Zimbabwe.detail: The government has started the process of blending diesel in response to fuel cost pressures.
